question.content: Compute the convolution output y(t)=x(t)*h(t) of the two given rectangular signals and write it in expanded unit-step form. Answer format: a fully expanded unit-step expression. Use only the variable names from the problem and ASCII characters; write explicit multiplication signs *; do not use bracket shorthands such as [u(t)-u(t-1)] or compressed grouped forms; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Method: unit-step algebra with ramp-kernel mapping. Express each rectangle as shifted unit-step differences and convolve algebraically instead of using overlap-length geometry.
step_1.key_point: New route uses bilinear expansion plus the identity u(t-a)*u(t-b)=r(t-a-b).

step_3.key_point: The convolution is reduced to four shifted unit-step pair terms.

step_5.content: Replace each pair by a shifted ramp r(t)=t*u(t): y(t)=6*(r(t)-r(t-1)-r(t-2)+r(t-3)).
step_5.key_point: This avoids piecewise integration and keeps a direct symbolic path.

step_7.content: Expand r(t-a) as (t-a)*u(t-a) to obtain the required fully expanded unit-step expression.
step_7.key_point: Expression format stays in ASCII with explicit multiplication signs.
